title: marketing — published macros
description: The marketing module publishes ZERO framework macros. Every consumer touches the marketing surface via injected services (MarketingEventDispatcher, MarketingProviderManager) or the #[AsDomainEventMapper] discovery path. Middleware attaches the AttributionContext (owned by growth::attribution) — marketing consumes it via injection.
rationale: Marketing is a SERVER-SIDE FORWARDER. The consumer contract is: fire a domain event → the mapper turns it into a MarketingEvent → the module fans out. There is no request-level state that needs to be surfaced as a Request macro (contrast with facility::activeFacility which caches a per-request model).

The attribution snapshot IS request-scoped — but it's owned by growth::attribution (via Request::currentAttribution / Request::attributionSnapshot macros). Marketing reads those via AttributionResolver injection, not via its own macros.
Adding a marketing-specific macro (e.g. Request::activeMarketingEvent) would signal a design smell — marketing events are ephemeral capture-then-fan-out artifacts, not persistent per-request state.
